diagrams.description: International Law is the body of rules, treaties, and customs that govern relations between sovereign states and other international actors. This flowchart illustrates its systematic functioning: the ultimate goal is to promote peace, order, and justice. It is built on primary sources (treaties, custom, general principles) and subsidiary sources (judicial decisions, scholarly writings). These apply to subjects (states, international organizations, individuals). The field branches into public international law (law of the sea, humanitarian law, human rights) and other areas (private international law, criminal law, economic law). Compliance is pursued through enforcement mechanisms (ICJ, arbitration, UN Security Council, sanctions) which together yield outcomes such as global stability, human rights protection, and peaceful dispute resolution.
